reviews.review_text: Really enjoyed my stay, felt like I was on set for the Indiana jones movies, loved the displays and the multiple spas/pools and pyramids. Very unique and creative place. The furniture in the room did need abit of an update it would have tied in nicely if they kept the theme going.  Steam room, gym and indoor pool were perfect for a long weekend re-set! Will definitely visit again.
Location and staff great, nice breakfast.  Disappointed in the run down state of the fixtures and shelving of our room, comfy bed. However,  not all room's are possibly like this.

We just wrapped up an amazing 4-night stay at Mantra Sun City Gold Coast Apartment #2703 (27th floor) and honestly, it exceeded all expectations! 🌊✨

The apartment was absolutely spotless on arrival — everything from the bedsheets, pillows, towels, napkins, and blankets felt fresh and perfectly clean. The kitchen was well-equipped with all the essential utensils and cutlery, making it super convenient for a comfortable stay.

One of the highlights? The TWO large, stunning balconies! We spent hours watching the blue sea and milky waves hitting the shores. We were treated to breathtaking sunrises and magical moonrises every day — and even lucky enough to spot a few rainbows during our stay 🌈🌅🌙 ..Also at nights enjoy the glowing skyline. Truly unforgettable views!

And guess what.. The apartment has a massive jacuzzi even! It comfortably can accommodate a family of three.

A special shoutout to Brianna from the staff — she was incredibly friendly, sharp, and helpful. She fixed our Netflix access on the TV within minutes, which made our evenings even more enjoyable.

The facilities were fantastic — a huge indoor heated pool along with sauna and steam rooms made it feel like a mini retreat. The location couldn’t be better: directly opposite the beach (just a 2-minute walk!) and with the tram station right outside, getting around was effortless.

We noticed Koastal Kitchen had a great breakfast buffet selection (though we didn’t get the chance to try it this time).

The undercover parking was convenient and secure, though it would be even better if the $20/day fee could be reconsidered.

Overall, an incredible stay with comfort, views, and location all ticking the boxes. We’d happily come back again! 💙

Rooms were nice but starting to get old but still very clean
Hotel is nice but gym doesn’t open till 7 which is to late
Don’t eat breakfast there it is absolutely terrible no decent coffee machine
All in all was a good place to stay just need a better kitchen and a bit of an update
Well located, so many pools and spas to choose from. Good carpark. Buffet breakfast was amazing, definitely worth a try!

Upon arrival staff were really friendly and polite, gave acknowledgement of Accor Status (Diamond Member). Gave us a room upgrade from hotel room to 1 BR Apartment. New F& B Manager was also friendly and good to see some new things happening in that regard with the bar being open longer hours and the new selection of Welcome drinks for Accor Members as was previously pretty poor, so very happy with the way things are changing. We love the spa and pool facilities here and have previously stayed multiple times. Have another booking in a couple of weeks for another 2 night recharge.

We loved our stay at Mantra Sun City. The rooms are big and many overlook the ocean. The place is extremely convenient with a tram stop right outside and an easy walk in to the surfers board walk. Staff at the front desk were so helpful - Bri in particular made sure we had everything we needed and was always so friendly. We will be staying again!

Great for families and convenient location. The hotel staff were great, the hotel was a little older and in need of upgrade in some areas, we were thankful to get a renovated room but bathroom was old and could use a renovation,  but we enjoyed the stay as it was great for kids which is why we chose it. Close to beach and tram, and Cavill Ave,  waterslides are a bit dangerous for little kids. But good to have the pools and spas for kids. Lots of noisy construction nearby so would not be great for a quiet adult getaway. Overall we enjoyed it.

Amazing location but not the best Frontline service.
Bit outdated

Still quite a nice hotel but it is looking a little tired in places. However, with the light rail (tram) on your doorstep it really is fantastic place to stay to go out and explore Surfers Paradise / Gold Coast

It was a lovely hotel with cool amenities but it was like staying at your grandparents, nice but dont touch or very strick rules surrounding things. Don't expect the hotel reception staff to be overly friendly either, felt like we were being a pain asking them anything hotel related. So in summary don't expect too much and you wont be disappointed. Also make sure you read all the extra things they may charge you for that other hotel offer in their packages

Love the theme. Good facilities. Good staff.
Overall great experience. We will be staying here again when we come back.

All rooms have different aspects.
The facilities are beautiful.

It was a truly beautiful stay. The 2 bedroom apartment was spacious, making our stay so easy and comfortable. The ease of living, prime location, and comfort was a blessing. We loved every minute of it and will definitely be back.
Fantastic place with the tram right outside and about 1km from the heart of Surfers. Ocean view apartments have an epic view. Inside pool is heated and the spa pool is nice and hot

We enjoyed our stay in the comfortable accommodation. The breakfast is really good with a great selection. Staff are friendly. Location is great with beach nearby and tram stop at the door.

Great facilities with the different pools, gym, steam room and sauna etc.  My most favourite thing was the Eastern Water Dragons, they are amazing.  We had great walks along the beach front which is very close to the hotel.  So many people out walking and running etc.  A very positive vibe.  Walking distance to eateries and shopping etc, otherwise there is a tram station right outside and only costs 50 cents per ride or $1.50 for a day pass which can get you to places and shopping malls etc.  Would go back for sure!!

Worst hotel ever staff are very rude and nasty to the guests when you raise any issues with them about thing's not working or being broken or stuff in general. This hotel refuses to fix any problems or sort any problems out that happen during your stay and they try saying there's nothing wrong when there is. The pregnant girl bree that works at the hotel and the other staff are very dramatic and make it feel like your doing them a favour when you ask them to sort stuff out when their very rude!! The hotel room is very old and outdated and looks nothing like the photo's that they have of the hotel rooms on their website!! The hotel rooms claim they are modern and nice but the hotel rooms look nothing like the photo's on the website and they don't even provide hardly any basic stuff at all. Multiple of the lifts were broken for the whole 6 day's of my stay and the lift wouldn't work at all or even go up floors and there was no other way to get up to the 16th floor when I told them about this they just rudely said they were getting it fixed or to try again and that it should work however it never got fixed and they got annoyed when I had to go back multiple times after multiple attempts of it not working constantly. The heated pool and spa pool also weren't even hot or luke warm they were cold. The hotel has a horrible disgusting rubbish smell that you can smell everywhere. My hotel room had multiple thing's that were broken and didn't work and if that wasn't bad enough they didn't even provide a blanket in my hotel room until I asked for it and the air con in the hotel room didn't work properly either and they expected me to suffer in the 35° heat!! This hotel is crap and definitely not worth the money it's over priced and a complete rip off I definitely don't recommend this hotel and would never stay here ever again
Great service ,great outdoor and indoor pool

The Mantra Sun City was a great place to stay, close to central Surfers.  Just a short walking distance to cafes and restaurants and opposite the beach. The two bedroom department was very spacious. The lounge had stunning floor ceiling windows with great views of the ocean and a connecting balcony that went along the side of the apartment, the balcony also had stunning views. The balcony was also accessible by both bedrooms. The master bedroom being the last bedroom in the apartment which was private. It had its own ensuite and walk in wardrobe. There was an additional bathroom with laundry facilities and it had a full kitchen. The ground floor pool is large and a great place to hang out with lots of sunbathing lounges to use m, it even has a mini sand beach and water slides. There is a downstairs restaurant.

I really loved the concept of the hotel, though unfortunately the hotel itself seemed a little old. The hotel was very elaborately decorated, with statues, fountains and other water features in the entrance and foyer. The pool was also pretty spectacular, just as well decorated, with two water slides and a 'beach' section with actual sand leading down into the water. The room I stayed in was very large, very good value for the price, and while the balcony on our level (level 5) didn't have much of a beach view because of the buildings in the way, it was still pretty decent and we could see a bit of the water between the buildings. The hotel did feel a little run-down though - for example, I noticed that the shelves in the bedroom were unstable, we were worried the shelves would collapse if we placed much on them. The balcony door from one of the bedrooms also wouldn't open, I don't know if it was deliberately sealed shut, but there was something blocking it from opening more than a couple of centimetres, and there was a very strong fragrance in the corridors that did seep into the rooms a bit. The location was decent enough, very close to the beach, though more like a 15 minute walk from the main parts of Surfers Paradise, and we didn't have any issues with noise, cleanliness or service.

The staff were extremely pleasant and lovely to deal with. Unfortunately the room was disgusting. I had a basic hotel room. The first room we were in, I found a used band-aid on the floor, the cutlery drawer was empty and filthy and the furniture was chipped. The latch to the door to the balcony was broken and you heard everything taking place in the bathroom next door. We were moved to a second room - the bed and sheets had what looked like blood stains all over them (including on the top sheet). It was so filthy and gross we ended our stay early. I knew the place would be "budget" standards before arriving - but the state of the bed and sheets was beyond budget. It was just plain unhygienic (the photos actually come out better than what it looked like)

We’ve just had a wonderful 9 days at sun city. The views, the pools and spas, the ease of access to the beach and the rail station right out the front all makes for a fantastic stay. we added breakfast, which was fantastic, easy and a great way to start each day. We were lucky enough to get a late checkout which was lovely. Thanks Sun City. See you next time. Matt and Tanya.

We had a great stay at your hotel. The staff at check-in were helpful and knowledgeable and the facilities were great. Our bed was very comfortable and the room was very clean and tidy. We liked having a balcony with a great view too. The buffet breakfast was great as well as delicious pizza and cocktails at the pool bar and cafe. The swimming pools were great. It was great having secure, undercover parking but it would be better if this was not at an additional cost.

Terrible service with rude staff at reception and the bar area. I said hello with "how are you going?" and was replied with "Hey". I asked for a welcome drink and was directed or pointed to a sign for welcome drinks with attitude.

Stayed at mantra sun city for 7 nights our third stay here. We love the location just up from the main street of surfers so you don’t hear all the noise & just a quick ride on the light rail to get to Cavil Ave which stops right at the gate of the hotel very handy & only .50c to ride the rail.
We had a 2 bedroom apartment which was nice & spacious & nice view of the beach. There was 4 of us staying in this apartment but only one chair out on the balcony the kitchen only had one frypan which if you wanted to cook bacon & eggs wasn’t ideal I did enquire at reception about getting extra chairs for outside & a frypan but was told apartment was privately owned that the owner would need to be contacted. The toaster also was so old that halfway through cooking toast it would start smoking.
The hairdryer in the bathroom has 2 setting if you used the high setting it turned off after 10 seconds could only use low setting & have to keep your finger on the button to keep it on its attached to the wall & not much cord to move around so had to stand very close to the wall.
Air conditioning works great in the lounge/kitchen/dining area the main bedroom not at all but there was a fan we found in the wardrobe so had to use that all night.
Beds & pillows very comfortable & great to have your own laundry to do washing.
You now have to pay $20 a day to park your car & are given an allocated parking spot.
Pool area is nice but the indoor spa wasn’t working.
Breakfast at Koastal kitchen was pretty ordinary but snacks & cocktails at the pool bar were nice.
Overall we enjoyed our stay & would  still stay again.
Loved the pools and spacious room

Stayed 2nd time with kids last month. We like it. Big apartment, comfortable beds, air conditioning, free unlimited wifi, underground parking, friendly staff, indoor and outdoor pools, spa’s, steam room, sauna, modern gym, tennis court. Beach two minutes away. Tram outside or ten minute walk to Surfers main mall. Be aware, their “ocean view” rooms no longer guarantee a good view. In our case, all we could see were new buildings under construction which sadly, once finished, will permanently block the view. The manager was very reasonable, offering to move us to something better when available or refund the difference between ocean and regular view. Other than that, parking is paid (not ideal but okay since we got a great room rate), the AC had one temperature setting for the whole apartment (no individual room adjustment), the TV was too small and needs an upgrade (which I let them know) and the indoor spa water stings the eyes (probably too many chemicals, though I note someone else online gave this feedback and hasn’t been addressed). Avoid the water slides, one of my kids got injured 1st time we stayed there. Overall, definitely recommend, will stay again

Overall, the stay was good. The facilities and location are great, and we really appreciated the birthday amenity.
However, when I called reception to enquire about pest control, Paula was quite dry and rude over the phone, there was no clear information provided. It would also have been helpful to be told that guests can cut through the hotel to access the tram station, especially as we weren’t driving.

Great location and facilities - especially the pools - kids loved these.  So close to the tram - easy access out the back of the complex.  Only downside is the state of the apartments - so run down and unpleasant - if these could be uplifted this place would be a 10/10.

We booked a 3-bedroom apartment. We checked in late at 10pm. The counter staff was not very friendly, but was able to provide us all the necessary information and details for our check-in. There is a room without aircon, which can be warm at certain times. The apartment was very spacious and self contained with amenities. It was clean. There was no housekeeping. So we had to reuse all our linens for our week's stay. The hotel is very convenient, a short walk to Surfers' Paradise beach and a short drive to all the attractions. We like the location and the floor we stayed. We managed to catch the New Year's Day fireworks from our balcony.

Staff are very helpful. The location provides fabulous views, 1 small block from the beach, and tram station at the entrance. The facilities are excellent with tennis, pickleball, and pools. Perfect for families of all ages.

Very disappointing experience.

I own a travel agency and booked this property for a family holiday and I would not recommend any of my customers to stay here in the future - there so many better options owned by mantra and other establishments in Surfers which are much better value for money.

The hotel needs a serious refurbishment, for the price point its extremely out dated and nothing seemed to work. From holes in the wall, to stained carpet, issues with appliances not working (including the vaccum, toaster, TV in the main living space didnt have a remote and was smaller then my computer monitor and not even a smart TV), even the shower stopped working mid use and needed to be fixed by maintence.

When i called reception to complain and try to be reaccommodated to another room, they advised that all the rooms are privately owned and rented out which is why there all fitted out aesthetically different....so its pretty much a lucky dip if you will recieve a nice room (if there are any). They also advised that they would not be able to move my family to a different room as the other room that was available "was not any better", so we unfortauntely we were stuck here for a week - but lessons learnt as we will not be back and neither will be any of my customers.

The only postive i have is the pool facilities were nice - which is the only selling point this property has going for it.

Most of the rooms ate tidy but could be updayed a little. Has everything you need though. Buffet breakfast is great too. There is a new construction right next door at the moment.

The property has a unique interior—antique yet modern at the same time. While some parts of the rooms could use a bit of renovation, everything was clean and comfortable. The facilities were impressive, with multiple pools, steam room, and a jacuzzi. And the buffet breakfast did not disappoint. Overall, a lovely stay!

Currently staying at the Mantra Sun City resort in Surfers Paradise, and wow, what a treat! 🤩  We are staying in the sub-penthouse on the 37th floor, and it is absolutely breathtaking.  Waking up to those ocean views every morning is pure magic. ✨  The apartment itself is so spacious and beautifully appointed, I feel like royalty.  Seriously, the decor is top-notch!  The balcony is the perfect spot for my morning coffee and watching the waves roll in. ☕️  Plus, the location is unbeatable, right in the heart of Surfers Paradise with everything at my fingertips.  I could easily walk to all the shops and restaurants, and the beach is just metres away! 🏖️  The amenities at the resort are fantastic too; I really enjoy the pool area. If you're looking for a luxurious getaway, I highly recommend the sub-penthouse here – a solid 5-star experience! ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ #SurfersParadise #SunCityResort #MantraHotels #SubPenthouse #OceanViews #LuxuryTravel #GoldCoast #HolidayGoals

I stayed here recently and the location was honestly the highlight — beautiful views and just steps from the tram stop, which made getting around super easy. The room was surprisingly large with decent facilities, and there was plenty of space to spread out.

However, the kitchen and bathroom were extremely outdated, and it really showed. Even the hair dryer looked like it hadn’t been manufactured since the early 1990s, which kind of summed up the overall condition. The hallway outside my room also had a strong vomit smell during my stay, which wasn’t great to walk through.

Overall, the location and views are fantastic, but the hotel desperately needs some modernising (and better cleaning) to live up to its potential.

We booked a few rooms at this accommodation for a family trip (2x 1brm and 1x 2brm). They were all really great sizes and with a weeks stay it was handy to have the washer/dryer. The views were awesome, all apartments were clean and the pool was amazing (we had lots of evening swims in the heated indoor pool) … it’s felt like an island resort!. The tram stops right outside which was super convenient (only 2 stops to Cavill) and we enjoyed happy hour at the bar for cocktails (pizzas were also yum). The beach is just down the road so we could easily walk down for swims as the flags were setup close by on the beach. We had an awesome stay and will most definitely be back!!
