Lovely clean appartments, and friendly service.

Absolutely amazing rooms and views, booked here for our family who came over from NZ they absolutely loved it.  They went to the pool, its huge! if there was more time we would of played tennis and basketball.  Highly recommend this place.  So close to the beach, the tram stop is just outside. There is a cafe downstairs.  Has everything you need

We had a lovely 2 night stay here.  Staff were helpful and friendly.  And whilst our room was very basic, it was comfortable, quiet and clean with a lovely river view.    The pool areas were great with lots of choice to relax and swim.  The breakfast buffet was generous and delicious in a lovely open setting.  Location was good too with an easy walk to the beach or shops and the tram close by.

We loved this property. Kids loved the pools and we were in them daily. Loved the surf lifesaver supervision they were friendly and on top of everything. All staff were great & we loved the location in the quieter area but still close to the beach.
Friendly staff and Conveniently close to everything
Absolutely wonderful, great location, clean room , great pool area and lovely staff
Great atmosphere in lobby and outdoor areas with Aztec theme. Unfortunately the room I had was a let down.
Good location, friendly staff and nice rooms.

Great stay for family groups. Our group of 2 families stayed in 2 x 2 bed units and found the experience wonderful. Big spacious units, easy access between our two units despite being on different floors. All we could want for a 4 night stay. Optimised tge daily breakfast which was a decent selection and plentiful. Great breakfast staff too!

family oriented hotel one bedroom apartment are great beautiful views and the lagoon pool is so beautiful and the hotel local water dragons are so cool lay around the rocks around the pool I have stayed there 5 times now so worth taking your children or even a solo trip.

The rooms are very tired, the bed in the main room was rock hard. We found rubbish under things so housekeeping isn't at its best. The bathrooms are clean but old. The doors don't lock and jam easy. Staff were friendly and the walk to the heart of the city is about 15mins.

Great pool and spa facilities. Really good for the kids.
Room out dated and needs refreshing.
Pool life guards were great, lady behind the bar not overly friendly. Staff generally looked like they didn't want to be there.
Great location, light rail stop out the front.

If the room had have had a microwave in it, it would have had everything we needed.  The pools, spas etc are amazing!  Lots of spots fo sit on your own, and spaces for groups like the bbq area.  Not crowded like I thought the facilities might have been.  Perfect place to keep kids amused too, you could live by the pool and slide area, with your feet in the sand if you wanted too.  Everyone working there seemed really friendly and helpful.  Would definitely recommend.

Having recently stayed at this hotel twice for work, please be aware that there are very different standards of rooms for exactly the same price. I have a colleague that has been given a lovely room but I have had awful rooms on both occasions. Old and dated is one thing (I’m not paying) but dirty is not acceptable and I have asked my company that we never use this hotel again. Please be aware of this if you are booking for a personal trip or vacation.
Lovely place to relax in Surfer Paradise

unit was Spartan. no soft lights for night. small tv. old carpet. old freedom furniture .i don't believe any money was spent on the unit since new. chipped wall corners everywhere. chipped and dirty paint everywhere.  |Just tired and worn out. I would not give the unit one star. it was crap. the rest of the so called resort 2 out of 5. and the staff... what a joke. we booked for five. one of the kids slept on the sofa. on the third night they delivered a bed. so resting on their laurels mentality. over all stay somewhere else.

The hotel facilities were fantastic and kept our 6 year old well entertained.  The 1 bed apartment was a great size also. We loved that the tram stop was literally on the door step of the hotel making getting out and exploring the GC so easy!  Definitely recommend staying here for families.
We had great communication from the staff before our stay via email and phone; then on arrival our room was ready; the staff were friendly and helpful throughout our stay.
We rented a two bedroom apartment. Holy moley, it was incredibly spacious and I would happily stay there again.

Great location, just north of the hustle and bustle of the main shopping and entertainment precinct.  Walkable to the main areas in Surfers Paradise, or easy access via tram stop just outside.  Quiet location when there is no construction work.  Amenities are fantastic, with both adults and children catered for.  Very friendly and helpful staff.
Nice and neat hotel, security was doing an awesome job, would give him a raise!
Nice place to stay - just need some improvements on the hangers inside wardrobe... simply things but will make major difference !

Our first room unfortunately had a strong chlorine smell. The staff were more than happy to help us out. Our second room was lovely. The beds were very comfortable, an excellent view from our room. Even though we were out every morning and evening for breakfast and dinner, we really enjoyed our stay at Mantra Sun City hotel! 🙂
Good location nice and clean hotel with parking available for guests. Friendly staff. Restaurant only opens for breakfast. Bar only opens for limited happy hours.
Great stay, had a stay with the family. While the hotel is a little older the place was clean our room was spacious and good. Friendly staff and good amenities onsite for the kids.
The desk ladies were very polite on arrival. Room was on the 20th floor. Beautiful views from balcony. Very quiet. Room was tidy and comfortable.
Not so great and very small rooms
Great location, very attentive staff.  Rooms are becoming out dated and could do with some renovations and the outdoor pool could be heated in winter.
Very happy - great facilities and plenty of space in rooms.  Easy access to public transport and beach.  Excellent price booked through Jetstar Holidays.

Great location, tram stop right outside the hotel.
We had our 16 month old baby with us, we put her cot in the walk in wardrobe.
Pool area is awesome for adults & children.

Stayed here on our family holiday. Breakfast was fresh and very nice. The pool facilities are on point. We were greeted by some absolutely amazing staff who helped us out whenever we needed it. The room had an amazing view and it was very spacious and clean. It would of been nice to get dinner at the hotel but That's not a big deal.
Very friendly staff and super accommodating. was a mix up with our room but the staff were very lovely and apologetic offering us reimbursements.
Fantastic location. Clean and spacious rooms. Great hotel especially as a family

Spent a week here to celebrate my wife's 70th birthday with the whole family. Very enjoyable. They looked after our every need.
Breakfast in their restaurant was excellent. They catered for 14 of us (9 adults and 5 kids).
All the staff at the hotel were friendly and extremely helpful.
The facilities are excellent and there is plenty to do there for everyone - even Pickleball!
Very good 的体验，180度无死角超美海景
Deal with anyone else but the assistant manager Davinder. He was rude, arrogant, unhelpful and displayed misogynistic behaviors. The opposite of customer service and an extremely poor role model.

On the day we arrived the staff member was rude and looked  tòo busy to deal with me.as she was on the internet.  We booked in and she never even gave us 2 card keys that worked. No laundry service what so Eva even though they advertise laundry.  Also our room never had a microwave as was on the book5

This hotel doesn't have proper bedding! It only has sheets on the bed and a throw rug in the cupboard. There are no doonas or quilts anywhere in the building, as you would expect to find in any standard hotel room!

The rooms are also not as modern as they look in the pictures! The bed is hard and has stains on the runner, the furniture appears to have been painted from the 80s to hide the wood finish. The feng shui or something seems to be out of wack in the room. There are way too many mirrors, it's hard to find a  power point, the desk is attached to a wall that you face and there is nowhere to open your suitcase other than on the floor or the bed. The WiFi is a router in the room and I had to call the actual hotel to access the WiFi password as it wasn't included  with my room key in the space provided and there is no number or hotel guide book on how to call reception. A very weird and underwhelming experience! There are also chips in the paint on the ceiling. I feel like I'm really slumming it for the price. On the plus side the shower is spacious and it's in an ok location.

Sunday to Tuesday winter stay so the hotel was quiet. Bar was closed on Sunday and reception closed at 9pm. Light rail stop is right at the back door so easy to get around Surfer's Paradise. Breakfast Buffet had a good variety and was always well stocked. We will return.

We went on holiday to the GC and spent almost a week at this hotel. We had a lovely stay in a one bedroom apartment with a gorgeous view. Staff were very friendly and helpful. They even allowed us to check in early and check out late with no added costs. I would have liked a service done on our room at least once during our stay, though. I asked for fresh clean towels on our second night but was advised we have to wash and reuse the ones that were originally provided (as our room had a washing machine), which was a bit disappointing. The WiFi is absolutely terrible. The only area in our apartment it would connect to our phone was if we sat in the laundry. Beds and pillows were very comfortable, I slept really well every night. The pool areas were fun, and the spa was nice and warm. We even had a couple of drinks at the bar, too. I also liked that the front main doors lock before it gets too late (entry via room card swipe) for added security. Breakfast and coffee from the Koastal Kitchen were also nice. However, I would recommend that more gluten-free options be made available. Otherwise, it was a fabulous stay, and we will definitely be staying again.
편안하고 조용한 분위기 넓은 공간
Great rooms, service, easy access to public transport,  great facilities and the best place that i have ever stayed at in my life! I'll be back in the future.  Miss it already
The place was very good for our stay but we just have one feedback. Please include smart tvs in the room or at least any streaming services like google chromecast or fire tv.

What can I say
Me and my kids love coming to mantra sun city.
My kids love the outdoor facilities. However fletcher he is such an amazing host at the pool bar. However we can't forget reception had such an amazing experience during the Cyclone Alfred and after that Geri the assistance Manager made myself and my kids feel safe and secure, giving my kids the mum talk on how everything will be OK there was nothing to be scared of.

Nothing. Davinder the assistant manager refused my stay as I arrived with my 4 year old daughter and 12 year old son, reservation was in my name but as the card was a business card with a family members  name (same surname) he accused us of potentially charging back the $377 for two nights, we offered to send authority, give a second card, some sort of solution but instead late in the evening he refused us, sent us away and told us to contact booking.com for a refund . So far booking.com and mantra have not refunded us or sent any replies. Davinder is apparently the assistant manager and in all my years of travel have never experienced a ruder person than this bloke, it’s shocking he has this position of an assistant manager,

Great hotel. Room with balcony, comfy bed , nice bathroom. Best of all this hotel has many pools indoor and out, sauna ,2 spas , bbq area, gardens, lounges etc. 2 tram stops from Cavil but close to tram stop and beach. Quiet.
The ocean view isn’t as breathtaking as I remembered, but it’s still worth the price.
We'll definitely book again.
Love staying here love the outdoor facilities clean rooms and great people to deal with at the desk

We stayed in a 2 bedroom apartment. The kitchen was brand new, but the furnishings were very plain. It felt like leftover furniture from a second hand Vinnie's, with nothing really matching or the right size for the space.

For example, the TV's were extraordinarily old, not even 1080p, and I hadn't seen one in so long I had to check the back and sure enough,   xx it had "Mantra Spare" written on the back. They weren't smart TVs either so free to air is all you get, even though the WiFi is very good.

It's certainly not 4 star accommodation.

Having said that, the bedding was clean and the beds were comfortable. No doonas - which has been noted in other reviews which you'd expect at a decent hotel. We had to use the blankets that were stashed in the cupboards.

Bathrooms were original (30 years old), but the toilets had been replaced along with the cupboard doors. Otherwise the wet areas were clean and well presented with no mould.

The hot water pressure was really poor in our room, down to little more than a trickle which was annoying.

Staff were friendly enough. It's not purely a holiday hotel so you're mixing with owners and renters which makes it a bit of a strange vibe.

The services were plentiful, with indoor heated pools, sauna, gym etc, although the steam room was broken / not running. Very short walk to the beach.

Overall it was ok, and I think the price reflected the age of the building and the quality of the accommodation.

Very very poor service from assistant manager Davinder on front desk. My wife and baby had a late evening check in with a reservation made with our company credit card. On arrival she didn't have the card but offered to give another card as security, put me on the phone with Davinder to sort it out, being late you would assume it could easily have a security deposit held from a different card, or reservation changed over? Nope, Davinder's exact words "how do I know your husband won't later go and charge the funds back from the bank"
This is the assistant manager, and he sent my wife and baby away to find another accommodation and advised her to get a refund from booking.com
This is the assistant manager of this hotel. No solution, just plain arrogance that will make any persons blood boil!!

lovely resort, great views of the beach and sea. rooms are spacious and well maintained. good facilities except both spa pools are not as warm as i would have liked and there is no way to turn off the bubble jets, (they are on a timer) tram stop literally outside the back door which is great for travel. there are no stairs for guests like myself that don't like the elevator. overall great experience here.

We had a very comfortable stay at Mantra Sun City. We chose it for the indoor pool, which didn't disappoint. The room was clean, spacious and offered a great view of the ocean. Fabulous location, with the  tram right on the doorstep. Highly recommend.

Good:
- Excellent location
- Partial water view
- Relatively new bathroom
Bad:
Very dirty blanket. We arrived in colder weather and, unlike other hotels, you have to make the bed yourself. When I touched the blanket, it felt slimy and really gross. There’s only one bedsheet underneath, with no cover or top sheet, so your body ends up touching the blanket directly. If you are sensitive about cleanliness, this is definitely not the place for you - it seems unlikely they wash the blanket after each guest. But staff were helpful to provide us with extra bedsheets.
The walls are cracked. The room we booked looked nothing like the photos on booking.com. They called it a “Hotel Room,” but it’s really just an old studio with no kitchen and not even a microwave. Everything is rusted.

Fantastic family weekend at Coast during the Gold Coast Marathon.  The huge 3 bedroom apartment on level 22 accommodated our family of six perfectly.  The view from the living area was lovely!  The big lounge suite  and dining table fitted us all comfortably, and plenty of things in the kitchen to be self-sufficient.  We had a Birds Eye view of the Marathon, and just a block from the beach for morning walks.  The foyer/bar area has all being refurbished and we had a nice afternoon at Happy Hour.
Horrible management! Avoid!

3 night stay to attend family wedding.  Hotel is nice and clean with staff very friendly and helpful, in particular security who assisted us with late check in on Thursday evening and directing us where we can get snacks.  The rooms were clean with good amenities, however reasonably outdated as far as decor etc.  Bed only had sheets and no doona, with only an additional thin blanket available in the wardrobe.

When I arrived, my reservation was cancelled by the hotel a day prior. Then I spent 2 hours on the phone trying to find out the reason and it was because someone (manager) decided that my booking was a fraud because there was someone with my name and surname doing frauds. So here I am , straight from the airport with cancelled room booking (no one notified me about it either). I had to insist to stay in the hotel at which ever room available as it was 100% hotel’s fault. I was promised not to be charged extra and I was. Still waiting for my refund. I am extremely dissatisfied with how hotel handled it. I spent two hours on the phone on that day, then another two hours trying to get through to talk about refund. I ended up emailing you about it. Still waiting.

Our room was amazing. We booked specifically for an ocean view and boy did we get one! It was an amazing view. The room was nice and big, and clean. Only downside was the cockroaches. Unfortunately plenty of them but they honestly didn't really bother us too much thankfully. The shower was a little fiddly with the hot and cold temperatures but it was a good shower. Pools were nice, though the indoor one needs a little cleaning. I honestly can't really fault our stay. Staff were super helpful, the location was so good and everything was super easy.

Facilities and location are fantastic. Pools are great for families with numerous outdoor options including a spa and large pool with slides. If it’s too cold(as it was for us) there is a large indoor pool and spa to accommodate. The tram stop just outside the gate makes things easily accessible for Surfers Paradise and beyond.

Located on the northern end of Surfers Paradise, so it's a bit of a walk to the restaurants, shops and beach. Room was very clean. Furnishings in the room were very tired and should be replaced. Staff were lovely and very helpful.

I have been a regular for years and I have just come back from a 7 day trip. I honestly was mixed emotions this week. Check in was amazing young lady helpful and guidance of carpark was helpful. Unfortunately the cleaning of the room was not existing. Stains over lounge room floor had baby crawling, so scrubbed out myself. Linen was filthy in main room pillow cases had stains and sheets had dirt under cover. Walls had stains over the wall thought it was blood but chocolate marks. I had perfect view can not complain about that. But for paying $2000 for room, car parking disappointed.

Purchased an App deal through Accor for a hotel room. The room was overlooking the highway, was in need of refurbishment and had its share of maintenance issues. Staff were pleasant but we were disappointed with the allocated room. We have stayed a number of times previously and enjoyed our stays at the time.
Had the best time, great place and good atmosphere 👌

The apartment is spacious and includes a kitchen, washing machine, and dryer. I had to visit the reception daily to collect amenities, but the staff were friendly. A light rail station is conveniently located just outside the back door. Overall, the hotel met my expectations.

The location is good.
The whole floor smelt of cigarettes.  The furnishings of the room were very old and outdated.  They should separate Hotel guests and residents.
The staff and other facilities were nice.

We stayed for the 2025 seafire event. Lovely location with G-link right outside. Older style hotel but BIG rooms as a result. Excellent sea view which unfortunately will be partially lost due to Meriton developments apparent determination to deprive everyone of a sea view with their new buildings 👎👎👎
It was our anniversary so the time away from the hassle and bussel of work life made this short break a real relaxing time. Thanks

Great place to stay!! Really enjoyed the location. Pools are great. Didn’t try the main pool was told it was cold so didn’t bother.  Only small negatives I can think of  are the dryer door broken so dryer kept stopping. Couldn’t get ceiling fan working and couldn’t lock sliding door on balcony. Was in 28th floor so didn’t think anyone would break in. Staff all nice and friendly. Thanks for a great stay!
Perfect position, perfectly run, clean and the views…..
Very relaxing, great location and a beautiful view

Location is excellent to everything. Friendly staff and is always very clean and well looked after. It is my go to place to stay when I’m on the Gold Coast.
Recommend to everyone to stay there.
The lighting in the lobby is too dark and hurts your eyes at night. It ruins the ambience when waiting for your room. Rooms are adequate but you can see they had a few renovations.

Not one to post negative reviews but the most terrible experience here… there was a used COVID-19 test on the balcony and a stain on the throw blanket, and no doona cover or sheets. We spent so much money for the night and our own homes would have been a much more pleasant experience. Do not waste your money! Would never stay here again, so disgusting.

Currently staying here when i booked i booked a room with an ocean view so i thought. upon arrival i asked for early check in which they said was fine i waited 4 hours after they continued to say that they are cleaning it then it was they started 15 minutes ago shouldn’t be long then it was they need to finial check the room could be about an hour wait. i then got the call my room was ready. i then walked up into my room to find out that it was not the room that was pictured online and it was not a ocean room. i then went down to ask what happened and the assistant manager then said no i booked a studio i then proceeded to show him the photos of what it shows when you book and he said he can’t do anything about it but i can upgrade the room at my cost. I then agreed to upgrade it he was then sitting their and goes i can’t offer you the room now as other guest are staying in it from the 10th of may and that he couldn’t do anything about it so i have to deal with the room that they catfished.

I was amazed at how unorganized this hotel is. First glance amazing. The staff at reception are bland. Don't smile. Soft spoken. Judy vibe. Got to room , but outdated but that's okay. Got in shower bottom of shower was slimy with mould on shower doors. Wifi doesnt work unless I found one wall of room I had to put my back UpTo.
I had to ask at reception for new towels ,they were not allocated to us a few days in. I got looked at funny I had used the same towel for two days. I would appreciate a fresh one.
Not to mention the pool was shut , we hadn't been told we got this hotel for the pool. Another women was especially mad as she had booked it for her and her children for that reason looked like amazing pool. They had another pool around the corner. It was very gross, don't use your goggles.

Perfectly positioned with the light rail station straight outside. Park your car underneath and just leave it there take the light rail up and down the coast. Brilliant and cheap. 4 minute walk to the beach and the Esplanade. Staff are extremely courteous and helpful. Rooms are well maintained and comfortable.
Excellent service clean great location awesome parking great hotel

Rooms given didn't allow me access to internet as it was in the corner with weak strength. Had to use my Internet to access things. Had to request for towel myself by going down to the lobby each time. No housekeeping staff came to clean the room which is really not what I have experienced in other hotels like IBIS and Novotel. This was my first time stay at Mantra and was very disappointed. Wouldn't recommend people who are particular about the cleaning part of the room. The location of the hotel is perfect for anyone who wants to experience the night life with ease as everything is closer to the hotel.

Room 1801 could do with a little love. One of the blinds refused to close. We had wooden sliding doors and literally one of the doors was missing. Lastly there are no locks on the bathroom/toilet doors.

Where to start!! This is not a resort! Far from. The only good this is some of the customer service, prior to arriving and the location otherwise it's a complete dump and would avoid.

On Agodas website, the room stated that there was a sofa bed.  Which there was not.

There was a bad smell coming from near a locked cupboard, i asked receptionist what this was, they said it was the owners items. Honestly smelt like something was dead in there.

There was a beeping sound happening every ten minutes. I alerted staff the first night. They didn't do anything. Complained the next morning and they resolved the issue.

The carpets were very dirty and stained. Don't think they even vacumed before arrival.

The blinds had some missing so couldn't block the light out properly.

The sheets were disgusting, either pee or sweat I'm unsure but it was filthy. This was only noticed after cleaning on check out. My baby and I slept on this.

They provided a small metal cot for my baby which there was no place for her to move, which resulted in her sleeping in my bed as well. Looked like baby jail!

The images look nothing like what was being advertised.

I complained and they offered 50% off for the first night only. I asked for a full refund, or atleast the first night where I was ignored.

Very over priced hotel. Only good thing is the location. Otherwise a complete health hazard.

I've honestly stayed at better hostels before.

Ruined my partners birthday and babies first vacation! Ended up leaving earlier than anticipated.
A great resort with lovely and helpful staff
Great views. Great location. Kid friendly. Light rail at your doorstep and the beach only a block away. Excellent sized apartment with a great layout. Home away from home!

I stay every few months due to seeing a Dr for my son, staff are always accomodating, rooms are always clean and give enough space for kids to play, and the pool has a great play area for the kids along with two heated pools a spa and bbq area.Trams are at your doorstep if you don’t feel like walking.

Room was good and location of the property also good. Swimming pools were great and there was a lifeguard on duty.

Unfortunately the hotel is let down by disinterested staff both at the front desk and in the restaurant. Breakfast was very poor for the price, food clearly been sitting for a while and the manager was busy chatting at the kitchen door than assisting guests. Check in process the team were not particularly engaging or helpful could definitely be a more welcoming experience.

Apartment was huge- we appreciated an upgrade from 2 to 3 bedroom. Location is great- one block from the beach, a nice walk into Cavill. Tram stop out the front. Pools and spas were great. Rooms need some upgrades but were functional, clean and comfortable. Reception staff friendly and helpful.
6 night holiday with family of four, rooms were clean and functional. Kitchen was minimally stocked with plates/cutlery, but other than that everything else was fine.

In desperate need of an ungrade. Carpet fraying, everything in the rooms are incredibly old and warn out. Service was terrible, requested a cot which, in an email confirmation said was free, then they tried to charge me. Cot wasn't set up, had to request it, sent the cot without any sheets. Aircon in the master room didnt work, main lounge would be freezing meanwhile the master was just as hot as outside, told them 3 times it wasn't working, nothing was done. The level we were on had a funky smell in the corridor. So many better hotels in surfers, dont waste your money with this one.

We stayed on the 14th floor in a 2 bedroom apartment. The size was of the rooms was fantastic. My two teenage kids loved the pools , gym  and how close public transport was to the resort .
Nice quiet end of surfers
Would stay again

部屋が広くて開放的でベッドも良かった！
設備は少し古いけど部屋が広いのはやはりとてもいい！
眺めもいいし、プールは広いし人も少なくて使いやすかった。
キッチン設備もほとんど揃ってるので料理をしたい人はとても便利。スーパーに行くのも楽しくてトラムやバスですぐだし夜の外食はほぼしませんでした。

Took a while for check in, at least 15 mins for 3 people. A bit of a slow process. Would recommend giving the pool towels straight away rather than a ticket for it. Rooms were average on hygiene. Some picture frames had years of dust collected which sat above the table. Location was a bit out of the way. Pool and amenities were the best part
Slide was a nice touch! Lifts were very quick and well maintained. Parking should be free as it was not in CBD area. Nice views

部屋が広くて開放的でベッドも良かった！
設備は少し古いけど部屋が広いのはやはりとてもいい！
眺めもいいし、プールは広いし人も少なくて使いやすかった。
キッチン設備もほとんど揃ってるので料理をしたい人はとても便利。スーパーに行くのも楽しくてトラムやバスですぐだし夜の外食はほぼしませんでした。

Our room was beautiful, clean and spacious with lovely balcony and views. Would highly recommend! Staff are friendly and very helpful. Tram at the back entrance gate, very handy! Loved our stay here, will be back

Just our of the hussle and bussle of surfers but close enough to walk. Light rail right at the doorstep  2 stops from cavill Ave. Indoor and outdoor pool, spa and restaurant for breakfast. Great staff and perfectly located

Complete One Stop Service to Rest for short holiday to Movie world.
Apartment service..
2min from Tram Stop..
You can buy groceries in Metro in 2 stop ahead.
We booked 2 bedrooms apartment.
Huge and Comfy
The price is affordable for 2 families.

Love from Indonesia

Run down accommodation and really dated rooms. Avoid if you have children or are thinking of a family holiday. TVs don't support casting so no watching a family movie together. Started getting bitten by mites in our room. Reception said nothing they could do.

Visiting for a nearby conference, my colleague and I were on different floors so could not go to each others rooms as swipe key only access your floor - when it works. Both the same room/price, one on 15th floor stank! Don’t know if it was mouldy carpet or something but a dark and depressing room in-spite of “ocean views”. Room not really clean either, stains on bedspread etc.
Room on 30th floor 10 times better, light with nice furniture and no smell. It doesn’t seem right for two rooms same price to be so different.
Buffet breakfast basic - $32 plus extra for coffee was a hard no.

Checkin was awful, room not ready on arrival, told it would be put on priority and we would be called. No one called. No information provided or offering to take or store bags no assistance with parking a vehicle that was to tall for car park. Told to go to bar for welcome drink which was closed. When it eventually open we couldn’t charge to room as they stuffed it up. Couldn’t use lift as keys weren’t cut properly. Terrible at night as you get woken up to the neighbours plumbing.

We stayed a week after cyclone Alfred and credit to mantra and the Gold Coast as it was a beautiful place to stay and relax, right on the beach and a very short stroll to Cavill Ave we enjoyed our stay. The pools were great for the kids and with the heated outdoor pool great for us adults and the spa is very warm which was great after days out sight seeing. Being able to use the pools up to 10pm was great.
Read my letter emailed to Sun city ast manager
Good for families with young kids.
Very nice. Hotel.   Worth the money.
The room and the view were great. The only thing that was missing was a restaurant for dinner. Otherwise everything was amazing
What a great location. Tram station right out the front two stops to Cavill AVE. Awesome.

Wonderful stay which was extended due to cyclone.  Staff were friendly, helpful and pro active during a highly stressful time.  Any needs were met and the stay was made pleasant.  The rooms are completely self sufficient which was fantastic particularly during the cyclone.  Highly recommend.  The 4 star for the rooms was due to our first room needing some work couch springs had completely gone so was very uncomfortable and no underlay under carpet.

If you'd like a room where you can hear the neighbours turn off their taps and flush their toilets this is the place for you.
Not only can you stay abreast of their bodily movements during the night but every finale is accentuated by the sound of rusty valves squealing to a close.

This entertainment is provided at a continuous, yet intermittent, rate ,24/7 so you never get complacent about the effort management has taken to provide it to you.

The lovely Bridget was surprised when I mentioned the noise problem, which surprised me as it is obviously a systemic problem with water valves. She kindly helped me to another room; this one even noisier due to the winds during the night rattling the door, next door, like a drum. Bass drum that is. Almost as entertaining as my previous room but at 80db a bit toooo invasive.

So back down to my previous squealer, where, after three days, I can report that my neighbour has no problem with their bowels, particularly at 2 and 4am.

I suppose we can expect a reply here which informs us this is an unusual occurrence.  But that would be dishonest. This is systemic. (Literally)
Very great place close to the beach close to surface paradise Maine Centre
We found getting around very easy just out the back door to the tram.
Lovely stay with my partner, with many thanks to Alan in Koastal Kitchen for going above & beyond my expectations. We can't wait to come back.
Perfect location right on the tram stop and  5 minute walk onto the beach.
Super convenient location with a tram station right under your nose.

Really nice place awesome pools. Great looking place. Pretty  well close to everything. Staff were all wonderful except for 1 member of staff  who was really rude short and wouldn’t smile at all.

Did have a funny smell through the place.

I would for sure come back.

Would have given a 5 if it wasn’t for the 1 staff member
I've stayed at Mantra Sun City multiple times, always an awesome stay. Clean , cozy and we never want to go. Shout out to Lianna who was great at getting our booking sorted this time around 👏

Rooms a bit dated now and plates and utensils a little inconsistent, but overall a great place to stay with great pool.  I've stayed before and I will stay again.  My comments are not actual criticisms, but more a comparison between the mantra and a 5* hotel

We got the 3 bedroom, ocean view room. Its so spacious and yes it does delivery what was promised but at a very low standard. The whole apartment was out-dated and plenty need to be fixed. We have 2x adults and 4x kids (10 and under); this place just hit the bare minimum. 6kg washing machine and a dryer that takes forever to dry; there's portable clothes line but no pegs.

We stayed for a whole week and they gave us only 1x small satchel of dishwashing liquid, dishwasher powder and washing powder. And when I asked if we could return dirty towels and get a new one; "you have a self maintain apartment, then no as you could wash & dry the towels your self". We've been to plenty of self-maintained apartments and sorry this is definitely at the bottom of the list.

The reception area was nice, and had a resort vibe. However, when we went up the elevator, the whole floor had a putrid stench until we got into our rooms. The room was dated, smelly and the bathroom looked unclean, so I wore slide shoes in the shower to avoid standing on the tiled floor which had mouldy grout. There was no room cleaning for the 7 day duration of our stay, beds weren’t made and towels weren’t replaced.
We were placed in room 2001B which shared a door with 2001A where a young family stayed. The kids and parents screamed constantly, the kids kept opening and closing the doors and loitering in the joint space outside our room, unsupervised. This woke us up in the morning and was not relaxing.  Staff couldn’t change our room due to availability.
There was very little staff available, and no pool side service.
The best part about the hotel was the pool area. However the big pool had freezing cold water so we didn’t use it. The big pool should have heating similar to the smaller outdoor pool. The hotel is very conveniently located next to the tram stop.

We were locked out of our room for 2 HOURS with a flat battery in the door opener at night, 4 workers attended before they decided to call a locksmith to change the lock because the receptionist that attended the first time apparently rounded out the socket to change the battery in the door lock. The attitude of the staff was disgusting constantly telling us they would have it sorted out shortly. I spoke to the manager the following day and he offered me $50 compensation.
I was left to sit and wait outside our room with my 6year old son (soaking wet) in an unairconditioned  corridor for 2 hours and on an extremely hot night ...
The room was very run down with the carpet dirty, stained, smelly and was the oven. The doors in the bathroom are falling apart. I booked ocean view and  got a view of cranes on the construction site across the road. perhaps advertise as obstructed view.
Far from acceptable for a Mantra resort.

We had a ball we have been coming there for 10 years now love the atmosphere and all the staff Tanya gosling Ben Hartono for making our stay so pleasurable making sure everything was perfect love the upgrades in the hotel also loved the Spanish and Italian night at coastal kitchen even reception at front desk always have a smile on there faces massive pool with slides indoor pool spas sauna gym room  beach one block away kids absolutely loved it will be back again end of year can’t wait

I booked a One-Bedroom Apartment with  Ocean View and the receptionist had a great and patient attitude.
I did request for a higher floor on Booking.com and was arranged to 29th floor as a result, which was trustful and generous of the Accor manager.
Besides, you can leave your luggage here when you just check out and are waiting for your flight or switching to another hotel.
The ocean view is so nice and clear and overlooking the building from the balcony can be exhilarating.

However, at first late night, I came back and the room card worked fine at front door, lifts except for my room door, so I realised the sensor of the room door lock was malfunctioning, not even showing any lights.
Thereafter, I went down to the reception desk and no one was there but a minute later, fortunately there came a man and was willing to help out.
As a result, he said it happened because I did open the balcony window when I just checked in, but the weather got much worse, rainier and windier than I thought after I went out at 4 p.m.

Because it was extremely windy and rainy, which was impossible to go out to buy drinks, I went down to B1 (card scanned)
and there were a few vending machines selling chips, soft drinks, and energy drinks.

Moreover, the quilt on the bed was very thin that you can easily feel cold after hours; however, there were some blankets for more warmth while sleeping if you need them.

In conclusion, the location is so convenient that you can just go to the side door of the hotel and reach the tram station (surfers paradise north) directly but when you return, you need to take out your room card to scan the sensor to enter again.
Overall, I would rate it a 9.5 out of 10!

The pool is cool, lots of open space and cool features like the water slide, the indoor pool is great, its relaxing and not crowded. But the rooms are the worst in my opinion, good for a single night sleep, but on the second day there were cockroaches everywhere, in the bathroom, in the beds. If you have a family or kids I would not recommend until they have fixed this issue of mass amounts of cockroaches. Even a supply of bug spray for each room would have helped heaps. Also! the locks on the bathrooms do not work, leaving some awkward encounters between family members allowing for little to no privacy.

We stayed at sun city for over a week excellent hotel fantastic pools waterslide was hit with my daughter only thing I say with spa that young kids in there with out supervision
With no parent is dangerous.
We definitely will be back again in January next year
This fantastic place for family to just relax and enjoy swim  very beautiful hotel

Considering the price we paid for 6 nights, the room was extremely rundown and in need of renovation. The views were beautiful, Ocean Views as described. other than that, not very impressed with the room. The facilities are great for a family; heated pools, water slides and saunas. The afternoon of checkout we received a call letting us know that we hadn't returned the car park key, which we had returned.

The next day another phone call, saying we still hadn't returned the key and we would be charged. Reconfirmed the car spot number and was told there was a mixup as they were looking for the wrong carpark number. Third phone call saying that the carpark number I had confirmed was not returned either. I asked them to check the reception cameras to make sure that we had not returned the key and was told on the camera you cannot see us returning the key. I asked her to send me a screenshot of the camera footage to show that you can clearly see us only returning the room swipe. FOURTH phone call later she says sorry we made a mistake you did return your key and we messed up with the carpark numbers.

Before checking in, I’ve called to request 2 extra towels. It was not fulfilled when checking in.

The lid on the bottom of the spa bath in indoor pool area is broken and it cut my partner’s heel.

Parking is $20 a day and we were not aware.

Tho they were nice enough to offer a small white wine for my partner’s birthday.
Experience was great
Great service, rooms are big. Enjoy breakfast in the morning with great options.
Definitely I’ll book again

住在Mantra Sun City Resort真是一個令人愉快的體驗！🏝這裡有一個寬敞的海景陽台，每天早晨都能欣賞到壯麗的海景，讓人心曠神怡。🌊度假村樓下還有一個方便的有軌電車站，讓交通更加便利，不論是探索城市還是享受周邊美景，都十分方便。🚋房間非常寬敞，讓人感覺很舒適，無論是家庭度假還是朋友聚會，這裡都是理想之選。🏠Mantra Sun City Resort 提供了絕佳的住宿體驗，無論是設施還是服務都讓人滿意。👍~強烈推薦給任何尋求放鬆和舒適度假的人們！🌟🌴~

My check-in experience was smooth and welcoming, with friendly staff that made a great first impression. I particularly appreciated the spacious car park, the cozy lounge area, and the variety of activities offered. These amenities were definitely the highlights of my stay.

However, my experience with the room was disappointing. The dusting was not done properly, and I noticed several areas that had not been cleaned well. What was even more frustrating was the complete lack of housekeeping services. Despite staying for four nights, we never had our room serviced once.

The breakfast was another major letdown. I strongly advise against adding breakfast to your reservation. The cleanliness of the plates and cups was unacceptable. Every plate I picked up had stains and food marks, and it took a good five minutes to find a clean plate or cup. The glasses for juice were all cloudy and had marks on them. To make matters worse, the tea or coffee mugs had sugar sachet wrappers stuck to them each day.

While the breakfast options were decent in terms of food variety, the cleanliness left much to be desired. It’s clear that the management needs to focus on maintaining proper hygiene standards, as it seemed like the staff didn't properly clean the dishes after use. This is a serious issue that needs immediate attention.

Overall, while the hotel has some positive features, my experience was marred by cleanliness issues and lack of housekeeping. I won’t be recommending this hotel to others.

Stayed rm 3101

The views were incredible and the location spot on - tram stop right outside the back gate - which no one alerted us to on check in we just luckily worked it out.
The room was huge and made it a comfortable stay for our family of 5!
We were able to cook Christmas lunch and enjoy a relaxing day in the space and didn’t feel cramped at all.

The staff we encountered were helpful and friendly.

In terms of the actual room lots of little issues not total deal breakers however for the cost of the stay I did expect better and from such a large hotel chain:
- the walls are chipped and dirty throughout
- the fridge had missing and broken shelves - we reported to reception and they had it fixed straight away
- the toaster broke and was replaced from a 4 toast to 2 which is crazy for the size room
- the tv is tiny and we couldn’t connect our Google chrome as it was so old
- the beds a terribly made with sheets and crappy fleece blankets. What would be the fitted sheet, isn’t fitted and constantly comes off the beds.
-  one of the couches has a big dip in it, the underneath is clearly broken.
- the lights in the bathroom are shocking
- the room is reference for 6 people but there are only 5 chairs at the kitchen table
- the outside setting is a huge table that doesn’t even fit on the balcony and two mis -matched chairs and a pool lounge chair. Such a shame we can’t even sit out there and enjoy the view and fresh air - again a room for 6 with the ability to only fit 2 people outside is ridiculous

1. Late check in
We arrived on time for the check in time but the rooms were not ready. We had to wait for over 40mins for our room and there were a lot of people waiting in the lobby.
All we got was a short apology until we specifically requested for a compensation. I believe this should have been done even before we asked since they wasted our precious time.

2. Dirty, broken room
The couch and the chairs were broken, the kettle was also broken, and the kitchen floors were super sticky. Also dirty bed sheets.

3. Bad smell, old building

I CANNOT understand why this hotel is 4 stars.

Needs maintenance. (Room 1709) interior - Furnishings too dated, needs a major overhaul, walls need to be repaired and painted, carpets need to be replaced.
Musty stale smells in the room & hallway.  Extra light switch in the bathroom for entry from WIR.
Bed is very firm and the split in the middle (2 single beds joined) is annoying.
Word of advice to the owner of the apartment- when you're charging "Meriton" prices it needs to be "Meriton" quality.

Lives up to its 3 star rating. Rooms are average, but how much time do you spend in your room right? The pool area is awesome. They need a good restaurant but the light rail station is right outside the gate to get to surfers.

Perfect location.  The tram is at the side entrance to the hotel. So regarding safety, this is spot on.
The room is well presented

Positive experience. The only downside was the bar closing at 6pm. Most people returning back to apartments from being out during the day. Would have been nice if it was open longer.
Breakfast very expensive

Great location, with underground parking. The hotel is a little bit tired in terms of it's decor and the rooms are quite basic with an old style small LCD television in the double room I had booked. My AC wasn't working so the team came and checked it promptly, and then proceeded to upgrade me to an apartment which was an incredible space, so was very thankful and a good customer service experience - Thank you. The swimming pool and leisure facilities are really good at this hotel. Looks like a great hotel for families. Definitely recommend and will be coming back here when back in Aus.

Perfectly situated near tram line and 5 min walk to the beach. Hotel facilities were great especially the pools. Stayed with 3 children <6 and several adults in a 3 bedroom apartment. Apartment needed a little love but in no way detracted from the stay. Beds were comfy and kitchen well equipped. Definitely recommend.

We found the room and amenities to have everything we needed with a kitchen with oven, toaster, stove and kettle.  Even tea and coffee for breakfast. The Pool, waterslide, spa and sauna were great additions as well.
However the condition of the room wasn't up to par with what we expected.  The bedroom door handle was nearly falling off, carpet had stains on them and someone had burnt the iron into the carpet.
After checking out we were blamed for staining the carpets which stains were already there upon check in.  The maintenance should up their standards in ensuring their rooms are repair and not accusing the people staying there previous issues and damage. Lucky we took photos on day of check in, to prove the stain were not caused by us and we were able to get refunded our deposit.

Excellent Experience all round at Mantra Sun City. Can not fault the service, room and customer service we received. 6 fantastic days.
Would recommend it and will be back again.
superb  u can plan your stay here

Poor reception service
Rest was all great.

Perhaps just unlucky with a room where the phone didn't work, the a/c was set to one temp and fan speed, and the room next door was a permanent resident doing noisy renovations. Reception was however very nice and helpful. Very close to the beautiful beach. Don't drop ur room key off until u have exited the car park! I learnt the hard way.

I recently had the pleasure of staying at Mantra Sun City, and I was thoroughly impressed with my experience. The resort offers a fantastic pool area, complete with waterslides, a heated pool, a spa, and even an indoor pool with its own spa – perfect for relaxation after a day out.

I stayed in a 2-bedroom ocean view apartment, and it was spacious, clean, and very comfortable. The beds and pillows were a highlight – offering a great night’s sleep after a busy day. The apartment also had all the amenities needed for a relaxing stay.

The location was ideal as well, just a 10-minute walk from Cavill Avenue, or you can take a quick tram ride, with a tram stop conveniently located right outside the resort. This made it easy to get around and enjoy the surrounding area.

Overall, I had a great stay at Mantra Sun City and would definitely recommend it for anyone looking for a comfortable, family-friendly resort with excellent facilities and a prime location.

Has this room been serviced or cleaned before handing it over to us as guests? It's so bad and stinky, it's so extremely disappointing and disgusting.
Only one roll of toilet paper, no tissues in box, mouldy, soap scum and grime shower head, dirty curtains, rubbish left on bathroom vanity.
Shall I go on? Whata waste! I'll never be back! 😧
Customer service of gentleman upon check-in has been lovely. Thank you.

Great position clean safe resort has nice pools and spa's great little bar erea over looking pool great breakfast our room had ocean views which was spectacular one street over from patrolled beach tram straight out front no need to hire car..will come back here was a good stress free break..well done mantra.

Check out on the 7th November  ...been a few weeks now , rang them up asking been told it's  pending  still waiting on the refund...part the location and the room was good ...i would recommend to stay away from Mantra , never go back very sad and disappointed

Such an amazing place to stay, and Fletcher the wonderful bartender made the experience 100% worth while.
Definitely would return 💕💕💕
#fletcher4life

Five stars because of the cute bar tender Fletcher.
We love his energy and he's so sweet!!
Will be returning.
Good clean hotel with nice facilities

Lovely place to stay
Staff are lovely 🌹❤️
Couldn't fault it, had everything we needed and a very spacious unit. Pool facilities had something for everyone
Decor is very dated. Room not serviced.

I booked this hotel as I’m Accor Plus Gold member, booked it via the All Accor app.

Location is convenient, 15 mins walking distance to the centre of surfers paradise.
The room is quite old, but it was clean. The view was quite nice from my room, and they have a snack pack in the room for me as a gesture for Gold members.
Pillows are very firm and high as well as old, not very comfortable for my neck.

The bed however was okay, it’s towards to the firm side but I prefer firm bed anyway.
This hotel is more like apartment settings, so don’t expect it runs like a hotel, don’t expect too much for “services”.

The cafe staff are friendly, the front desk staff were average, came across one even a bit rude.

It’s definitely not qualified to be a 4 star hotel, but it was okay if you looking for cheaper options for weekends.

We had an amazing family trip staying at Mantra Sun City in Surfers Paradise! We booked a 2-bedroom apartment, and the experience exceeded our expectations. The apartment was incredibly spacious, with a large lounge area, laundry room, and a fully equipped kitchen—perfect for cooking meals and even hosting friends for dinner. The bedrooms were a good size, and the master bedroom came with a walk-in wardrobe leading to a luxurious bathroom featuring two sinks, a shower, a toilet, and a bath.

The highlight of our stay was definitely the breathtaking views from the balcony, especially during sunrise. You get a perfect blend of ocean views and city life—just stunning! The apartment also had access to three fantastic swimming pools: one indoor heated pool with a spa, and two outdoor pools with water slides and a great seating area with BBQ facilities. The kids absolutely loved it!

The location was also ideal, only a 5-minutes walk to the beach, restaurants, and a supermarket. We enjoyed a delicious breakfast each morning, with a wide variety of food options to suit everyone's taste. Overall, Mantra Sun City was the perfect spot for a family vacation, and we’ll definitely be back
Awesome 👌

She's old and everything is broken in this Hotel! Had a few rooms as all the families on holiday together, every door handle in every room was broken or jammed, holes in the bathroom doors where the locks use to be. Wifi unit didn't work in one of rooms and so many other things just old or broken. Minimal staff around, but the pool is awesome and the light rail stops right out front which is super handy.

We stayed here last week for 3 nights... pretty dowdy...very old rooms..need revamping...paint pealing off on desk..bathroom very 70's.. holes & stains on the curtains... would not recommend...also they charge $20 per night for you to park yr car in their carpark!!.

Excellent place, in a prime location.

But be warned there is no free car park spaces anymore.

And they are building a very tall building right at the front, so most sea views will soon disappear

My daughter and I stayed at the Mantra Sun City.  We had a very pleasant stay.  The only disappointing aspect of our room was that when you went to go into the room, there was a very strong smell of Indian cooking from the next door neighbour's apartment.  I like Indian food but the aroma smelled so strong that we held our breath when walking past the neighbour's apartment.  Apart from that, the apartment was well appointed, clean and spacious.  Friendly staff.  Beautiful looking premises.
Beautiful rooms and tram stop right outside.

Cheap hotel in poor condition.
Staff were friendly enough.

Dirty,  stained carpets and in a state of disrepair.

Fantastic place. Central, clean and so handy. Tram right out the front, bus right out the back. 2x stores within 3 minutes walking distance. Several pools, slides, outdoor spa and chill places with free BBQs. Great for families with children. We air bnbd there.
Spacious apartments right in the heart of Indy track. Tram stop at door, awesome pools onsite. Reasonable views of ocean.

I absolutely loved this hotel as far as it has the tram right on our doorstep. The rooms are tired in need of some maintenance. Was worried when they said they put a $200 bond on my room in case of damage. I took about 30 photos of damaged areas in my room and was worried about being charged for it. Had a family room, so fully equipped kitchen (Leaking fridge), laundry, lounge tv, good bedroom, bath with shower and toilet. Check when booking that your room isn't next to a privately owned room. As you could get a shock when you hear a barking dog in the next room. Fantastic swimming pools, spa, sauna, and steam room. Overall I enjoyed my stay and I will come back.

Based on location  we chose this hotel  to hav good experience.  Unfortunately  we cant make it due to sickness .
As a brand and expensive accomodation its expected to be flexible enough  to consider some rescheduling whereas our all money wasted.
So book wisely , same range one can get  other 5 star hotel where  services has more flexibility.
Very nice hotel, with a huge room and very good amenities, including pool and sauna. Right next to a tram station so easy and quick transport links to anywhere in the city
Very helpfull stuff lovely apartment train right side wood stay again
Loved my stay at the mantra - beautiful location and amenities. Steven Webster from the front desk was an absolute legend helping me with my reservation too
Really outdated, needs a refurb. Noisy next to the road and light coming through so not the best night sleep
Great place to watch the Pacific Air Show from our balcony on the 31st floor. Loved it!

It's OK if you enjoy mouldy bathrooms, broken kitchen tiles covered with thick black tape. Mouldy ceilings and six inches of dust in the crevices of every single part of the carpeted outskirtings. Blown light bulbs, an odd odour coming from the bathroom, kitchen overhead that has flaking paint falling into your food and you'll still be charged $88 for a few marks left on the carpet that they'll state is because you didn't leave the apartment up to sellable standards.
Definitely wouldn't recommend the pools, especially the indoor pool + spa. The spa water is so thick it looks like gelatin and if you dare step into the indoor heated pool, you will instantly slip and slide. Definitely need some better maintenance, we stayed 5 days and not once was anything in the pool areas cleaned.
The building is not new but comfortable to stay here.

The table has mould, kitchen benches are chipped  floors dirty and blinds filthy
We paid a lot of money for the Bates motel.
I feel extremely dirty and peeved that I'm staying in conditions par to a hostel .
Also found cockroaches in the bathroom
Why don't you have pride and clean these rooms?
Why put a moulding table for people to use?
Why or how are you even an operational business?

Went here 5 years ago, was good back then. Now it’s old and dirty.

Our sheets were dirty, had to ask for them to get changed.
Our bathroom smelt like a sewer!! Said they would send someone up, no one did anything.

Buffet breakfast is an absolute joke. 5 years ago it was awesome and a great range of food. Now it’s not worth the money. Didn’t have any gluten free sweets. Just some bread.

4 year old child in the pool with floaties on, jumping in and out! Screaming and yelling. Had 2 younger sisters with him. Reported it to reception don’t think they cared.
Next day more children alone in the pool, going under in the spar bath and spitting out water, disgusting.

We won’t be coming back here ever.
Insects in the rooms even on high up levels

We had a great week of winter sun here. Room was spacious and well set up for all we needed it to be. Spa, sauna and steam room were great.

We ventured down for breakfast once and would not do it again - not worth the price and the range of food available was unhealthy and poor quality.

We only stayed here one night. The service was the absolute best. It looked so beautiful driving up into to forecourt and the foyer was stunning. We got up to our room and it was a pretty big anticlimax, it smelled bad like cat piss! we were only staying for a night so we just opened all the windows. Main room bed was dreamy and we slept soooooo well. The two beds in the other room also very comfy. Extra bed was no more than a camp bed and boy was my daughter wild!! The sliding doors to the balcony never opened so that was dumb. The rooms were alright though in general the apartment was old and dated in comparison to the gorgeous forecourt and foyer. Thoroughly enjoyed the spa and pool area

This is not a hotel. The units are individually owned. It's obvious that Mantra does not hold owners accountable to any basic hotel standard. Our room stank of food cooking next door, all of our belongings also stank as our entrance was shared with the unit next door. My eyes were burning from the onion fumes. The room was run down, the AC about 30 years old, the tv small.
Front desk staff were accommodating and able to move us to a different room but no refund for the abhorrent room provided for the first 2 nights. Scam at best.
***Avoid room 1902b at all costs. *** I would advise avoiding any a or b style room as you have to share an entry door with other people and you can hear their conversation through the door.

I will be avoiding all Mantra managed buildings in the future.
We were a party of 11 people, with four different rooms booked. We spent thousands of dollars for our 7 night accommodation. The discrepancy between rooms is appalling.
Mantra, please inquire about providing a refund.

Patrons please note Mantras reply makes no actual effort to rectify the situation.
The staff at reception have been fabulous. They helped me out of a jam when they didn't have to - so thank you! Appreciated 😊
Location is great

Not a bad place . Highlights Pools & Location . Lowlight definitely the dated room and poor cleanliness. Felt like we were back in the 90s on the 17th Floor . i would rate the cleanliness 4/10 - marks all over the place . Bed terribly uncomfortable. If i am being honest Money probably better spent staying  somewhere else . Love the GC but was hard to come back to our room after each day .
Couldnt even get towels when requested. Gold accor membership and no drink on arrival, no discount at the resturant and welcome pack was some bars.

Lobby Service was fast and friendly, Apartment well sized with a great sea view, just a bit dated.
The seal around the toilet had a leak and the bathroom floor got wet but once reported a technician arrived very fast.
Breakfast costs $25, service was not ready between 8.00-8.30am, only a handful of fresh hot food options which were empty and came out very slow.
Coffee from a coffee machine had to be paid extra.
So weird to find a South American themed resort in Australia. I've had some lovely times here and encountered zero crackheads on site.

The Mantra Sun City property is made in the design inspired by the Sun City in Africa. The lobby are pool area is decorated with statues and my kid had enjoyed the lagoon area a lot. It has steam and Sauna available for the guests with Jacuzzi bath.
Dev Chaudhary was very helpful in guiding me about the tours and plans.
Beach is walkable and I had enjoyed the early morning jog while i stayed in this hotel.
We had booked a standard room earlier which was smaller and compact but later on upgraded to a service apartment which had amazing beach view and at night one can hear the waves splashing from the varandah. The one BHK flat on the 16th floor was equipped with utensils, toiletries, etc...

One limitation is that this hotel doesn't have a restaurant for lunch or dinner. only breakfast is served.

Beautiful stay! We loved our stay. Close to the tram and surfers paradise strip. Near coles & woolworths.  3 pools & 2 spas. We enjoyed all the facilities sauna,gym & steam room. + bonus Our apartment was gorgeous.

Wish there was room service with a hot food menu. Plus breakfast buffet needs more meat selection (sausages etc) for breakfast.

Overall we had the best time. Thank you.
Very good service for my service dog and I
It was a great time away. Staff were great room was neat and tidy
Needs a coat of paint and a bit more but you can't argue with that view. Note: not all rooms are this fancy, be careful to get the bigger room when you book!
Good location and facilities.  Hotel room is very old, need renovation.

Absolutely love the location and the poolside bar is just awesome and the breakfast buffet is so delicious I went back for seconds. The views are spectacular and management are friendly. Highly recommended 👍👍👍
Great find for a self service apartment
A favourite accommodation of mine with awesome pool facilities
Excellent resort
Great room
Very good location, amazing pools, big rooms - only flaw is that there's no room service or restaurant.

Very nice Apartment with great views 16 stories high,Sunrise and Sunset was visible but they don't clean your room unless you stay more than a week.
The Pool was great with 2 water slides included and also offered a indoor pool,Sauna and a outside jacuzzi which was so relaxing after a long day out in the sun.

Super convenient location right outside Surfers Paradise North tram stop. Staff were super friendly and let us put our luggage in a holding room on the day we arrived prior to check in whilst we went out. Rooms are nice and super clean and have a balcony. AC is fantastic in the rooms. The pools and spas are fantastic for a warm day. The only thing I wasn’t a fan of was the buffet breakfast as it didn’t have much of a variety for $20 per person.

地點在Surfer paradise的邊緣，從最主要的Cavil Ave走到旅館有大約需要15分鐘，兩站輕軌站的距離，但是旅館的後門就是輕軌站，累了不想走也可以搭輕軌。房間很大，風景很好，工作人員友善，每天的早餐樣式都一樣，味道中等，如果下次再入住，我應該不會選擇添加這個選項。整體的氣氛很好，划水道也很有趣，但泳池的磁磚很鋒利，我老公的手被刮到傷口有點深，所以流了很多血，救生員有過來緊急止血，只是他後來幾天都無法玩水了，所以這方面可能要注意一下。

The most dreadful accommodation I've ever experienced; steer clear at any expense. Opted for this place for our first trip in three years with my partner, and it was nothing like the advertisements!

The initial room assigned to us was in shambles; the walls showed evident neglect with peeling paint and chunks of missing plaster, and marks marred every surface. Ripped carpets, mold on the bathroom ceiling, and shower glass added to the dismay. The bathroom door was warped due to moisture damage, and the furniture and units were worn and covered in marks.

Seeking a change the next day, we found the manager, Davinder, to be about as helpful as a chocolate fire guard. He claimed the hotel wasn't responsible for room conditions as they are owned by third parties, making repairs contingent on the owners' willingness to foot the bill.

After much persistence, Davinder asked us to return later that day to check for an alternative room, but the replacement was only marginally better. The air conditioning unit was missing fins, preventing adjustment, and the outside unit was rusted and emitted loud, disruptive sounds, especially during the summer in Brisbane.

Adding insult to injury, the promised breakfast was closed on the second day due to staffing issues. A letter in each room offered a refund, and when it finally opened the following day, only one functioning toaster served the entire hotel. Both pancake machines were either off or broken, a recurring issue throughout our stay. On the third day, when it finally opened (late), the beans and bacon were cold, and the bread stale, leading to stomach problems that confined us to our room for the entire day. After this ordeal, we avoided the breakfast and dined out for the remainder of our trip.

Despite filing a complaint about the room's condition and the breakfast debacle, Davinder insisted nothing was wrong and refused to compensate for our stay.

Beware and stay far away from this hotel at all costs.

The room we stayed in was via Air Bnb and was amazing. Views of the Ocean was surreal. Location was ideal, was only a 2 min drive from Caville Avenue and a 2 min walk to the beach. Indoor and Outdoor pools and spas were convenient. Disappointed that the restaurant on site does a buffet  breakfast only and not a very good one at that. Would highly recommend staying here.

Great location and lovely service. Better to book directly rather than a third party as you can get membership points and need to be signed up to their loyalty programme to access the free wifi. There is no microwave but has a kettle.

Thank you so so much to the manager/assistant manager who was so wonderful and helpful throughout and for the team who was happy to fulfill my request for high floor and even upgraded my room! It has been a weekend to remember and I am really thankful to the team at Mantra Sun City. Thanks for the outstanding customer service and hospitality. Cannot recommend this resort enough

When I approached reception and asked for an extra towel so people didn’t have to share, her response was that we didn’t have have house keeping services and to put the towel in the dryer between uses so it dries. Very dissapointed they wouldn’t allow enough towels for all guests. Would recommend staying elsewhere if you’re wanting a nice hotel-like experience, or bring your own towels when you stay 😊 The blankets on the beds are also very obviously unwashed between guests, but the sheets were clean.

Checked into mantra sun city for a 5 day stay, on the first evening we were given a faulty room which anyone could access as the lock on the door was broken and could be accessed without the key. Tried to raise this with reception who told us that we had no other option but to stay in the room and wait for another to become available even after expressing concerns around safety this was dismissed and we were asking “I don’t know what you want me to do?” “We have no other option”. Secondly, this place calls themselves a hotel but rooms do not get cleaned everyday, the beds do not have duvets, just a throw for a couch from Kmart in each room which is meant to be a blanket. Lastly, as the rooms don’t get cleaned everyday, this means that you do not receive fresh towels or toilet paper. After going down to reception to ask for clean towels for our room, we were told that we had to pay $5 per clean towel. Everyday. Absolutely ridiculous. Do not recommend this place.

Rooms are dirty, mouldy and the couch was stained. Outdoor pool is great for the kids, but they charge for pool towels. No wifi, one mink blanket for bed(unwashed). We were prior guests, but have found the place has become really rundown. Will stay elsewhere in future and I recommend anyone else do the same.
